name: BUBUN KUGUTSU NO JUTSU (PARTIAL PUPPETRY TECHNIQUE)

description.value:
Type: Ninjutsu

Rank: 2

Chakra Cost: 1

Learn DC: 16 | Successes: 3

Perform: 4 | Perform DC: 15

Components: C, Mas

Similar to Kugutsu no Jutsu, the user is able to animate a single limb or part of a puppet in range. The puppet from which the limb or part comes from must not be animated or currently under control of another puppeteer. The user must have at least one hand free to control and concentrate on this technique, and controlling a puppet limb counts as if the user is controlling a small puppet on the same manner as Kugutsu no Jutsu, counting on the limit of puppets that can be controlled at the same time. If the part has any weapon or offensive component attached to it (Such as a limb with the claw component, or a tail) the user can use the limb or part to attack. However, instead of using the Puppet's attack bonus, the user's standard ranged attack bonus is used. The user does not apply his strength bonus to damage. The user can activate any component currently installed on that limb or part, as normal.

The object always has normal hit points and hardness for that kind of object, a chakra pool of 1, and cannot move further than 30 feet from the user. If the object or the puppeteer are attacked, the user must make a Concentration check (DC 15+damage dealt) or lose control of it. If the object's chakra pool or hit points drop to 0, it becomes disabled and cannot be animated again for 1d4 rounds. If the puppet limb or part is sundered or destroyed (normal rules for sundering or destroying objects apply normally), it is destroyed and must be repaired. The object has a Defense score of 10 + Size modifier + the user's Dexterity modifier + the user's class bonus to Defense -2. This technique benefits from the range and perform bonuses granted by the Advanced Puppetry I ability.

CHAKRA JIRAI NO JUTSU (CHAKRA LANDMINE TECHNIQUE) Ninjutsu

Rank: 4 (B); Learn DC: 18, 3 successes; Perform: 6 (DC 19); Time: 1 attack action; Components: C, H; Range: Close; Area: 5-ft. square, 15-ft. radius burst (see text); Duration: 1 minute/level (or until discharged) (D); Save: Reflex half (see text); CR: Yes; Cost: 5.

Using this technique, one is able to set a trap that reacts strongly to high Chakra signature and emanation. Upon completion of this technique, the user choses a single 5-ft. square to host the landmine. Once a creature with a Chakra Pool greater than 5 enters the chosen space, the explosion will be set off. All creatures within a 15-ft. radius centered on the chosen space suffer 4d6 point of force damage from the explosion, which can be halved with a successful Reflex save.

In order to locate a landmine, the person searching must make a Search check, DC equal to the saving throw of the jutsu upon being performed. Those who can Sense Chakra get a +2 to the Search DC to locate a landmine. Those who can See Chakra or See Through Chakra are able to perceive landmines normally unless properly concealed.

Requires: Kugutsu no Jutsu

Empower: The user may spend 1 point of chakra to increase the damage by 1 die, maximum 1 die per (up to 1d6 per level or 10d6).

Mastery: The first step of mastery in this technique grants the user +1 to the Search DC of the landmine.

The second step of mastery in this technique allows the user to change half the total die (rounded down) to an element they are capable of using.

The third step of mastery in this technique increases Search DC of the landmine to +2.

The fourth step of mastery in this technique allows the user to change all the die to an element they are capable of using.

The fifth step of mastery in this technique increases Search DC of the landmine to +3.

The fifth step of mastery allows the user to try and attempt to conceal the chakra signature of the land mine with a standard Chakra Control check to Conceal. If successful, those with See Chakra and See Through Chakra will need to make Search checks as normal. This also negates the bonus from Sense Chakra.
